title: Node Fetch, Redirect Control, Streams, Timeouts, and Bounded Bodies · Guided Contract Studio
summary: A disability-led public-interest technology team incrementally builds bounded Fetch response adapter from original deterministic HTTP, robots, sitemap, HTML, browser, and report fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, web, Git, accessibility, security, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of bounded Fetch response adapter while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pra…
objectives: Set honest User-Agent, Accept, conditional validators, safe methods, credentials policy, and manual redirect handling through one adapter.
Classify transport, status, media type, length, encoding, validator, and domain outcome before parsing.
Count compressed, decoded, consumed, and retained bytes during stream processing and cancel over-budget bodies.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Fetch Request Contra…
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 9124 during an authorized municipal service audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove honest Fetch requests, response admission, streaming byte limits, composed timeouts, and cleanup; every Promise, stream, listener, timer, socket, file, browser context, and report sink needs one cleanup owner; then reorder one frontier, p-limit, Promise, AbortSignal, stream, retry, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Set honest User-Agent, Accept, conditional validators, safe methods, credentials policy, and manual redirect handling through one adapter.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Fetch Request Contra….

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Fetch Request Contra…, keep Fetch Request Contract connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.
